scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Kaushal Unnayan and Vaishvik Rojgar Yojana
page_title: Mukhyamantri Kaushal Unnayan and Vaishvik Rojgar Yojana

details: The “Mukhyamantri Kaushal Unnayan and Vaishvik Rojgar Yojana” was launched by the Department of Skill Development and Employment, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide skill upgradation training along with country-specific training to the youth of the state, enabling them to secure employment opportunities abroad.
benefits: Under this scheme, youth of Uttarakhand are provided with skill upgradation training in various domains such as language, culture, work ethics, nursing, and hospitality, along with country-specific training.
The training is provided as per the demand of foreign employers to enable the youth to secure suitable service and job opportunities abroad.
eligibility: The applicant must be a youth of Uttarakhand.

online: Youth interested in overseas employment can register on the
. The registration process requires basic details such as the preferred field of work, educational qualification certificates, and passport information.
After registration, applicants will be invited for a screening test. Those who qualify will become eligible for training.
Upon selection, candidates must submit their Aadhaar card, bank account details, and permanent residence certificate. Training may be provided in either residential or non-residential mode.

The State Government bears 20% of the total training cost. If the trainee avails a loan for up to 60% of the training cost, the State Government will subsidize 75% of the loan interest. In cases where no loan is taken, only 20% of the cost will be covered by the State Government. Trainees can apply for the loan during the training period, with assistance provided by the concerned department.
After completing the training, the employing company or institution conducts a test or interview. Only candidates who pass will be sent for overseas employment.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Any young resident of Uttarakhand can apply for the scheme.

faqs.question: Which domains are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Training is provided in domains such as language, culture, work ethics, nursing, and hospitality.

faqs.question: How can youth register for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Interested youth can register on the Apuni Sarkar portal by providing basic information, educational certificates, and passport details.

faqs.question: What happens after registration?
faqs.answer: After registration, applicants are called for a screening test. Only those who pass the screening are eligible for training.

faqs.question: How much of the training cost is covered by the government?
faqs.answer: The State Government covers 20% of the total training cost.

faqs.question: What if a trainee takes a loan for training?
faqs.answer: If a loan is taken for up to 60% of the total training cost, the State Government will bear 75% of the loan interest.

faqs.question: What if no loan is taken?
faqs.answer: In such cases, the State Government will cover only 20% of the training cost.

faqs.question: When should the trainee apply for a loan?
faqs.answer: The trainee can apply for the loan during the training period.

faqs.question: What happens after the training is completed?
faqs.answer: After training, the employing company or institution conducts a test or interview to assess the candidate.

faqs.question: Is the registration process open throughout the year?
faqs.answer: Yes, registration on the Apuni Sarkar portal is continuous.
